metaDescription: Learn how to download and import your Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) into TurboTax in 2026, plus fixes for common import errors partnership filers hit.
intro: If you receive income from a partnership, getting your Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) into TurboTax accurately is one of the more finicky parts of tax season. This guide covers how to download your K-1, import it into TurboTax, and troubleshoot the import issues that commonly trip people up. It's written for individual partners as well as tax professionals handling K-1s for multiple clients.

steps.heading: Understand what Schedule K-1 reports
steps.body: Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) reports your share of income from a partnership, and knowing what it covers helps you catch errors during import.

steps.heading: Download your K-1 form
steps.body: Get your Schedule K-1 from the partnership or financial institution issuing it before you can import it into TurboTax.

steps.heading: Import the K-1 into TurboTax
steps.body: Use TurboTax's import feature to pull your K-1 data in directly rather than entering every figure by hand.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common import issues
steps.body: If the import doesn't go smoothly, a set of common issues and fixes helps you resolve the problem instead of resorting to manual entry.

steps.heading: Optimize your overall filing experience
steps.body: Beyond the import itself, tips on integrating TurboTax with your accounting software help streamline the rest of your partnership income filing.

faq.question: Where do I get my Schedule K-1 to import into TurboTax?
faq.answer: Your K-1 comes from the partnership, S corporation, estate, or trust that's reporting your share of income, and you'll need to download it before importing.

faq.question: Why is my K-1 import failing in TurboTax?
faq.answer: Import issues can come from a few different sources, and the tutorial walks through common troubleshooting tips to resolve them.

faq.question: Do I still need to manually check the imported K-1 data?
faq.answer: Yes — importing reduces manual entry, but reviewing the imported figures against your actual K-1 form is still a good practice to catch any discrepancies.

faq.question: Is importing a K-1 different for tax professionals managing multiple clients?
faq.answer: The process is the same, but the video notes this feature is especially useful for professionals managing several clients' K-1 forms efficiently.
